text: Here I find myself wandering through dimly lit corridors, the silence pressing in, broken only by the distant hum of electricity. I encounter signs of intellect and order, reminders of purpose, but the absence of people amplifies a sense of isolation. Am I on the threshold of discovery or merely haunting the remnants of past ambitions? In this emptiness, I feel both curious and unsettled, pondering what lingers in shadow and what calls from the light beyond glass doors.
